Ad Text:
A2B Australia is part of the ComfortDelGro Australia (CDC) Group, the country’s leading taxi network and a major provider of mobility, technology, and payment solutions.

Through our iconic brands 13cabs, Silver Service, Swan Taxis and Cabcharge we deliver end‑to‑end transport experiences, from booking and dispatch through to safe, reliable travel and seamless payment. Our services support corporate, community and disability customers nationwide.
Our network is Australia’s largest, with 7,000+ vehicles and 11,500 drivers, supported by contact centres, technology hubs and in‑field support teams. We power drivers, operators and passengers with dispatch, navigation, in‑car tech, apps and payment solutions across Australia and globally.
  
The A2B Way
We care about our customers, our business and each other. We work as one connected team, value accountability and collaboration, and encourage innovation to shape the future of mobility. We invest in wellbeing, safety, learning and development, recognise high performance, and celebrate diversity and inclusion.

The Role

We’re looking for a talented Insight Engineer to join our growing data team within a fast‑paced taxi and payments business. This role sits at the intersection of data engineering, analytics, and emerging AI, playing a critical part in turning complex data into trusted, actionable insights that support smarter business decisions.

Working closely with business stakeholders, analysts, and data scientists, you’ll design and deliver scalable data pipelines, translate business needs into robust data models, and enable advanced analytics and AI use cases. This is a hands‑on, high‑impact role where your work will directly influence how the business operates, grows, and innovates. 

What you’ll be doing

Design, build, and maintain scalable Azure data pipelines and platforms across Data Factory, Synapse, and Data Lake
Develop and optimise data models supporting data warehouse, big data, and Data Vault architectures
Deliver reliable batch and streaming data solutions, ensuring high data quality and performance
Translate business requirements into curated datasets and semantic layers that power reporting, analytics, and decision‑making
Partner closely with stakeholders to generate actionable insights from complex datasets such as payments, trips, and customer behaviour
Implement CI/CD and infrastructure‑as‑code practices to improve reliability, scalability, and cost efficiency
Support emerging AI and advanced analytics use cases, working with data scientists on feature engineering and model deployment (where applicable).
About you
 
We’re looking for an experienced Insight Engineer who has operated within enterprise‑scale organisations, managing complex data environments and working across multiple stakeholders. Our ideal candidate brings experience in data or analytics engineering, with strong hands‑on expertise across the Azure data stack and a solid grounding in data warehousing, data modelling, and ETL/ELT best practices. You’re comfortable working with high‑volume, business‑critical data, translating insight requirements into robust technical solutions, and partnering closely with the business to deliver reliable, scalable outcomes.

Skills and experience

Required 

5–7+ years experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or insight engineering roles  
Strong hands-on experience with Azure data stack:  
Azure Data Factory  
Azure Synapse Analytics  
Azure Data Lake
Solid understanding of:  
Data warehousing concepts (star schema, dimensional modelling)  
Big data processing frameworks  
Data Vault modelling 
Advanced proficiency in SQL, Stored procedure, PowerShell,  
Experience with real-time/streaming data (e.g., Kafka, Event Hubs)  
At least one programming language (Python preferred)  
Experience building and maintaining robust ETL/ELT pipelines  
Strong understanding of business data and ability to translate insight requirements into technical solutions  
Ready to apply?

If you’re an experienced Insight Engineer looking to work in a complex, enterprise environment and make a meaningful impact through data, we’d love to hear from you.

Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

ComfortDelGro Corporation Australia (CDC) and A2B acknowledges the Traditional Custodians throughout Australia, and their continuing connection to land, waters and community. We pay our respects to all First Nations people, their culture and to Elders past and present.

$200k - $250k base + super

Build and scale a healthcare platform using AI-first engineering

Hybrid role


Note: This role is focused on relational database architecture — schema design, optimisation, and large-scale migrations — not data science, analytics pipelines, or ML/AI model training.


At vital.ly, our engineering scope is broad and complex.

Externally, our proprietary customer platform supports over 14,000 healthcare practitioners. It enables wholesale ordering for clinic dispensaries, advanced prescribing workflows with built-in clinical safety features, and digital prescriptions sent directly to patients for online fulfilment - all powered by Australia’s most comprehensive complementary medicine reference tools.

Internally, the proprietary platform is the backbone of our distribution business, turning prescriptions into deliveries. It provides customised workflows and tools that manage practitioner access, inventory, orders, and reference content as well as protect patient data - making this ecosystem uniquely powerful.

Connecting every part, internal and external APIs ensure seamless integration, while AWS infrastructure delivers security, resilience, and scalability.

As we evolve the platform, we are undertaking a critical transformation of our data architecture — moving from a legacy MySQL schema to a structured, scalable PostgreSQL environment, while embedding AI-first engineering practices into how we build.




Who this role is for:

You've spent years inside relational databases — arguing about index cardinality, diagnosing query plans, and adding foreign keys to production tables without downtime. You've migrated between database engines and you know the hardest part is never the data — it's the implicit assumptions in the application code.

This is a principal engineer role from day one. Your first major workstream is owning the MySQL → PostgreSQL migration end-to-end. Once that lands, your focus shifts to platform architecture, data modelling, and technical leadership alongside the Head of Engineering.

You think in terms of systems, not just features. Data integrity, performance, and long-term maintainability matter to you, and you take a structured approach to solving problems that others may consider too complex or risky.

You are comfortable working independently and taking ownership of a critical workstream. When something isn’t ready, you can clearly explain why — and back that up with sound technical reasoning.

We build with AI-assisted development tools (Claude, agentic workflows).

This isn't an ML role - we expect you to be an efficiency multiplier, designing workflows and guardrails for the team and pushing the boundaries of AI-amplified engineering. If you're still hand-writing every line and plan to keep it that way, this isn't the right fit.




The Responsibilities


Phase 1 (Schema Formalisation & Migration)

Design and operate agentic AI workflows (tools, prompts, guardrails) to accelerate schema auditing and migration tasks.

Audit the full MySQL schema, including tables, columns, indexes, implicit relationships, and type/charset/collation inconsistencies.

Add foreign keys and constraints to a live production database without downtime using Laravel’s migration tooling.

Resolve data inconsistencies, including orphaned rows, type mismatches, and ambiguous columns, in collaboration with founding engineers.

Plan and execute the MySQL to PostgreSQL migration end-to-end.

Document all schema changes with migration files, clear rationale, and rollback plans.

Coordinate migration sequencing and provide clear, consistent progress reporting to the team.


Phase 2 (Platform Expansion)

Partner with the Head of Engineering to uphold high standards of data architecture and optimisation across the platform.

Define and refine AI agent rules, roles, and guardrails to support AI-first engineering practices.

Review merge requests and authorise production releases.

Own the search architecture, including tsvector FTS, postGIS and pgvector embeddings.

Design and build platform features on the migrated architecture.

Own modules end-to-end across the service layer, APIs, and frontend where required.

Optimise query performance, define indexing strategies, and guide ongoing data modelling across the platform.

Shape technical direction and architectural decisions as the product evolves.




Requirements

Must haves:

Deep expertise in relational database internals: storage engines, locking behaviour, transaction isolation, index internals, and collation/character set optimisation. You should be able to explain why a query is slow, not just that it is.

Proven experience executing large-scale data migrations (millions of rows) with batching, progress tracking, validation, and safe rollback strategies.

Architecture-level engineering experience, including API, modular monoliths, and contract-driven design.

Experience with AI-assisted development workflows and a genuine enthusiasm for pushing their boundaries - including reviewing and validating AI-generated output for production quality.

This is a permanent, full-time role and requires unrestricted and ongoing work rights in Australia.

Nice to have:

Experience with CDC processes and MySQL binlog-based replication or change data capture mechanisms.

Experience working with pgvector for embedding storage and similarity search.

Experience optimising PostgreSQL full-text search using tsvector and related indexing strategies.

Production experience with AWS infrastructure and services, including ECS, RDS, CloudFront, Secrets Manager, EC2, and CloudWatch.

Experience in healthcare or other regulated environments.

Hands-on experience with PHP/Laravel

Experience building custom developer tooling - MCP servers, CLI tools, workflow automation.




About vital.ly

vital.ly is the infrastructure behind sustainable clinical practice. Trusted by over 14,000 practitioners across Australia, vital.ly integrates three pillars of infrastructure — technology platform, distribution capability, and practitioner expertise — to transform how clinics prescribe and dispense complementary medicines.

Through vital.ly, practitioners can access wholesale ordering from leading brands, dispense prescriptions digitally, and use advanced prescribing tools with built-in clinical safety tools. Every order is fulfilled through our own dedicated warehouse, with fulfilment operations designed for speed, accuracy, and scale. Behind it all is a team of qualified practitioners and experienced professionals who provide the support and guidance needed to practice with confidence.

Joining vital.ly means becoming part of a business that empowers practitioners, improves patient outcomes, and drives innovation in complementary medicine.

About the role

Embed Tomago Aluminium’s values of Teamwork, Achievement and Care through inclusive leadership, accountability and everyday behaviours
About you
You are a technically capable and pragmatic automation engineer who enjoys working in a fast‑paced, asset‑intensive environment. You bring strong problem‑solving skills, a commitment to safety, and the ability to work collaboratively across technical and operational teams.
You take ownership of your work, communicate clearly, and thrive on improving systems, processes and outcomes.

Essential Skills and experience

Demonstrated commitment to safety and risk management in industrial environments
Relevant industrial automation, electrical or engineering tertiary qualifications, or equivalent experience
Exceptional problem‑solving and critical‑thinking skills, with the ability to resolve non‑standard issues

Experience delivering or supporting industrial projects across the full lifecycle

Why Tomago Aluminium
Tomago Aluminium is a globally competitive manufacturer of primary aluminium products and one of the industry’s most dynamic and innovative producers. Based in the Hunter Region for more than 42 years, we offer long‑term stability, modern technologies, and a strong commitment to safety, sustainability and operational excellence.
Our people are transforming the business for a sustainable future — and you’ll be part of a collaborative team that values quality, safety and continuous improvement.
Employee benefits

Competitive salary with generous annual incentive program
16 weeks paid parental leave for primary and 2 weeks for secondary caregivers (after 12 months’ service)
Free onsite gym facilities
Employee Assistance Program (family access)
Onsite free parking and canteen
We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds.
Preferred candidates will be subject to drug and alcohol testing as part of the pre‑employment medical, in accordance with our Fitness for Work policy.

AI Governance Specialist

Sydney, NSW | Contract

The Opportunity

A leading NSW government organisation is seeking an experienced AI Governance Specialist to lead the development and implementation of enterprise-wide AI governance practices. This is a high-impact role focused on ensuring the safe, ethical, and compliant use of Artificial Intelligence across a large, complex environment.

You will play a critical role in shaping governance frameworks, embedding risk controls, and driving organisational adoption of AI policies-working closely with Legal, Privacy, Ethics, Procurement, and ICT teams.

This role is advisory and assurance-focused, influencing outcomes across the business. It does not involve building or delivering AI solutions.

Key Responsibilities
Develop and maintain AI governance frameworks, policies, and standards aligned to NSW and global best practices
Lead the AI risk management process, including system classification, risk assessments, and treatment oversight
Embed AI governance into procurement and contract management, including supplier obligations and evaluation criteria
Partner with Legal, Privacy, Ethics, and Procurement teams to develop AI-specific contractual clauses and guidance
Coordinate ethical and privacy reviews, ensuring outcomes are captured in governance artefacts
Establish and manage the organisation's AI system register and assurance processes
Monitor evolving AI regulations and standards, advising on impacts and required changes
Provide reporting, insights, and assurance to senior executives and governance forums
Lead AI change and adoption strategies, including training, communications, and capability uplift
Develop practical resources such as playbooks, FAQs, and micro-learning content
Deliver training on AI safety, ethics, privacy-by-design, and responsible use
Track and report on AI adoption, compliance, and governance maturity metrics
Act as a central point of contact for AI governance queries and escalation pathways
Build and maintain a network of AI champions across the organisation
Key Relationships

Internal:

Chief Data Officer & Data Governance Office
Legal, Privacy, Ethics, Procurement & ICT teams
Project Managers & Product Owners

External:

NSW Government stakeholders and assurance bodies
Vendors and suppliers (governance & compliance oversight)
About You

You are a senior governance professional with deep expertise in AI, data governance, and risk frameworks. You're comfortable operating in complex environments and influencing stakeholders at all levels.

Key Requirements:

10+ years' experience in Data and/or AI Governance
Proven experience implementing governance tools such as Collibra, IBM Watson, Credo AI, Informatica, or similar
Strong background in policy development, risk management, and procurement governance
Hands-on experience with AI risk management, including system classification and risk assessment frameworks
Strong knowledge of frameworks such as NSW AIAF, NIST AI RMF, ISO/IEC 42001, ISO/IEC 23894
Experience embedding ethical AI practices (bias mitigation, transparency, fairness)
Demonstrated ability to partner across Legal, Privacy, Ethics, Procurement, and Data teams
Excellent stakeholder engagement, communication, and influencing skills
Why Apply?
Shape AI governance at scale within a large, complex organisation
Work at the forefront of AI policy, ethics, and regulation
Influence executive decision-making and compliance outcomes
Drive meaningful change in how AI is safely adopted

Job Title: Machine Learning Engineer - Deep Learning Specialist (Computer Vision)
Ad Text:
Company Description


Property intelligence is reshaping how the world understands the built environment, and Nearmap is driving that. We put powerful aerial imagery, AI-driven analytics, and geospatial tools into the hands of the people who plan, build, insure, and govern the places we all live and work. Our technology turns property uncertainty into decisive action, and our culture brings out the best in the people who build it.

Job Description


We are recruiting a hands-on Machine Learning Engineer with a passion for R&D and strong expertise in applying deep learning techniques to practical computer vision applications. You will work with an incredibly passionate and talented team of Data Scientists and Machine Learning Engineers, and your work will have a real impact on Nearmap products. You will train novel deep learning models that leverage our rich data sets, multiple dates of high-resolution imagery, multi-angle source imagery and 3D textured mesh. You will collaborate closely with other team members to solve various 2D and 3D vision tasks by selecting the model architecture, designing training and evaluation methodologies, generating a suitable dataset, and optimising models for deployment. The released models will run on millions of square kilometres of Nearmap imagery.

Experience in applying deep learning techniques to commercial use cases and non-academic problems is highly valued. We are committed to software best practices including infrastructure as code, GitOps, CI/CD, and as much automation as makes sense. A strong background in 3D modelling is advantageous in this role.

Key Responsibilities

Design and scope greenfield machine learning projects in collaboration with the team
Develop and deliver end-to-end solutions to complex technical problems
Train and optimize deep learning models using our extensive multi-temporal, multi-angle, and 3D imagery datasets
Participate in technical discussions, code reviews, and knowledge sharing sessions

Qualifications


Mandatory Skills

Programming/Tech Environments: Ability to code in scientific Python, using a Linux environment, and Git for source control
Machine Learning: Strong grasp of machine learning fundamentals (regularisation, hyperparameter optimisation, validation methods), and recent AI advancements
Scientific Approach: Follow the scientific method of formulating hypotheses, and applying statistical tests to validate them
Deep Learning: Applying modern artificial neural networks to solve machine learning problem
Tertiary Qualifications Formal education in a field related to computer science, machine learning, deep learning and AI.

Highly Desirable Skills

Domain Knowledge – Computer Vision: Working on Machine Learning problems applied to image data
Domain Knowledge – 3D Reconstruction: Experience with 3D computer vision, photogrammetry, structure-from-motion, or related technologies
Software Engineering: Working on shared codebases to produce production quality code
Cloud Computing: Working on AWS or GCP using distributed virtual machines, Docker containers, etc.
GP-GPU: Using GPUs to accelerate scientific computing
Scale: Working with large data sets, where data sets don't fit into memory, and require multiple nodes to compute efficiently

Personal Attributes

Pragmatism: While extensive knowledge of ML theory is highly valued, we prioritize pragmatic solutions that work over elaborate theory when shipping products
Collaboration: Data science is a team sport—communicate well, share knowledge, and be open to taking on ideas from anyone in the team
Attention to detail: Thoroughness in model development, testing, and documentation

This role is based in Sydney, Australia but also open to candidates from the east coast of Australia working remotely.

Please note: Visa sponsorship is not available for this role. Applicants must have permanent rights to work in Australia.
 

Additional Information


Some of our benefits

Nearmap takes a holistic approach to our employees’ emotional, physical and financial wellness. Some of our current benefits include:

Quarterly wellbeing day off - Four additional days off annually for your 'YOU' Days
Wellbeing and technology allowance
Annual flu vaccinations
Hybrid flexibility for this role 
Nearmap subscription (of course!)
Stocked kitchen with access to all the snacks you need
In-office lunch every Tuesday and Thursday at our Sydney CBD office
Showers available for anyone cycling to work or lunchtime gym-goers!

Working at Nearmap

We move fast, we care about craft, and we're proud of what we're building. If you're energised by turning hard problems into real-world impact, we'd love to meet you

Read the product documentation for Nearmap AI:

https://docs.nearmap.com/display/ND/NEARMAP+AI

For a deep dive into Nearmap AI, listen to AI Systems Senior Director Mike Bewley on the Mapscaping podcast https://mapscaping.com/blogs/the-mapscaping-podcast/collecting-and-processing-aerial-imagery-at-scale

Company Description


Property intelligence is reshaping how the world understands the built environment, and Nearmap is driving that. We put powerful aerial imagery, AI-driven analytics, and geospatial tools into the hands of the people who plan, build, insure, and govern the places we all live and work. Our technology turns property uncertainty into decisive action, and our culture brings out the best in the people who build it.

Job Description


To help us scale the AI systems behind that intelligence, we're looking for a Senior MLOps Engineer to join our AICV Platform team in Sydney.

This is a genuine opportunity to own greenfield platform work at serious scale. Nearmap is one of the top three AWS users in the APAC region. The systems you'll build aren't theoretical — they're processing aerial imagery across Australia, the US, and Canada, and serving AI models to real customers at high concurrency.

You'll sit between traditional DevOps and the new world of AI engineering — which means this role suits someone with a strong back-end and platform engineering foundation who is ready to grow their career into AI, or who is already making that transition. AI experience is a bonus, not a barrier to entry — we can coach that side as a team.

🔹 What you'll be doing

You'll own projects end to end — from architecture and design through to implementation, delivery, and production operations. That kind of trust and autonomy is real here, not a recruitment line.

Day to day that looks like:

Designing and building scalable ML infrastructure — large-scale batch inference on EKS, real-time model serving via Ray Serve, and GPU-accelerated distributed training on GCP and AWS
Building the LLM platform that will underpin Nearmap's next generation of generative AI products — including deployment, governance, experimentation, and cost optimisation across CPU/GPU workloads
Owning developer experience for AICV engineering teams — CI/CD pipelines, Docker tooling, ArgoCD, W&B, and Arize AI
Driving reliability and observability across production ML systems with real SLAs and SLOs
Contributing production-quality Python to shared platform libraries and services
Playing a tech lead role on your primary project, with advisory exposure across others
Engaging directly with ML tooling vendors including AWS, GCP, Weights & Biases, Confluent, and Arize AI

The teams depending on your platform include Model R&D, Computer Vision, Insurance Data Science, Agentic AI, and AI Map Data.

Qualifications


What we're looking for

Must-haves:

Production experience building and operating Kubernetes-based platforms (EKS/GKE) at scale
High-scale, high-concurrency API development and operation in production environments
Real operational mindset: you've managed systems with SLAs and SLOs and stayed close through incidents
Strong DevOps fundamentals — CI/CD, infrastructure-as-code, deployment pipelines
Production-level Python — services and libraries, not just scripts

Highly valued:

Ray Serve or equivalent real-time model serving experience
LLM or generative AI platform work in production (beyond PoC)
ML observability tooling — Arize AI, Evidently, Prometheus/Grafana
Infrastructure-as-code with Terraform
Experience at terabyte or petabyte scale
GPU/CPU cost optimisation

A degree in computer science, engineering, mathematics, or a related field is welcome — equivalent practical experience is equally valued.

This role is based in our Barangaroo, Sydney office, with an expectation of at least two days per week on-site.

This role is open to Australian citizens and permanent residents, or those with full working rights in Australia.

Additional Information

Wellbeing and technology allowance

Stocked kitchen with access to all the snacks you need
In-office lunch every Tuesday and Thursday at our Sydney CBD office

About Us

Beck Water is a growing national water infrastructure business committed to solving water challenges with reliable systems, transparent service, and local expertise. As we grow, we're investing in smart technology to work smarter — and we're looking for someone to lead that charge. This role sits within Beck Water however carries the potential to extend across our broader group of companies, giving the right person significant scope to grow their impact.

⚠️ This role is based on-premises on the Gold Coast. Please do not apply if you are not currently living close to the Gold Coast — remote and hybrid arrangements are not available for this position.

The Role

This is a hands-on, exploratory role for someone who gets excited about finding inefficiencies and fixing them with practical AI solutions. You won't be handed a list of tasks — you'll spend your first months understanding how we operate, then design strategy (including things like security polices for AI, in conjuction with management) and deliver automation tools that make a real difference: smarter reporting, email workflows, task management, chatbots, and more.

You'll work primarily with Claude as your core tool — and we mean properly across it: Claude Code, Cowork, and hands-on vibe coding of real applications. You'll remain open to integrating other platforms where they best fit the problem.

What You'll Do

Months 1–3: Embed yourself in the business. Shadow teams, map processes and policies, and identify where time is being lost.

Months 4–6: Deliver weekly quick wins — automations and AI tools that teams can start using immediately.

Beyond: Own and lead larger transformation projects across Beck Water and potentially other companies within our group.

What You Bring

You're fully down the rabbit hole with Claude — actively using Claude Code, Cowork, and Claude-powered workflows as part of your daily toolkit, not just experimenting

Demonstrated experience vibe coding and shipping real applications — we want to see what you've built

Hands-on experience with AI workflow and automation tools including N8N, Make, Zapier, Relay, Activepieces, or similar — you know when to use which tool and why

Practical experience with low-code integration platforms such as Zapier, Make, or Relay — minimum 2+ years

A minimum of 1–2 years hands-on experience with LLM platforms, with Claude as the preferred benchmark

A degree in IT, Computer Science, or a related field — or equivalent hands-on experience

Genuine curiosity about how businesses work and where technology can improve them

The ability to translate technical ideas into clear recommendations for non-technical stakeholders

A bias toward action and a track record of getting things shipped

Openness to working across different business contexts as the role evolves

Australian work rights are required for this position

What We Offer

A real seat at the table — your ideas will be heard and implemented

The opportunity to shape how an entire group of businesses uses AI, from the ground up

Variety — exposure to different industries and challenges as you grow in the role

A collaborative team that values initiative and curiosity

Are you a veteran of the Data and AI landscape with a passion for the ethical frontier of technology? We are looking for a visionary AI Governance Specialist to lead the charge in establishing a world-class framework for safe, ethical, and effective Artificial Intelligence.


In this high-impact role, you won't just be following rules-you'll be building the blueprint for how a complex, large-scale organization adopts AI. You will bridge the gap between cutting-edge innovation and robust integrity, ensuring that as we move faster, we move safely.

The role:
As our Ai governance lead you'll develop and maintain the policies, risk controls and change strategies that define our AI journey.You will act as the master orchestrator between Legal, Privacy, Ethics, and Procurement, ensuring every AI initiative is backed by rigorous assurance and transparency.

Key Responsibilities:


Architect governance:Develop and maintain AI policies aligned with global standards like ISO/IEC 42001 and the NIST AI RMF.
Manage risk:Lead the classification of AI systems and oversee treatment plans to mitigate bias and ensure fairness.
Empower the organisation:Create the "Playbooks" and training programs that translate complex regulations into practical, everyday guidance for our teams.
Strategic advisory:Provide expert evidence and insights to senior executives and external regulators.
Build a movement:Establish a network of AI Champions to reinforce a culture of responsible and effective AI use across the agency.

Your experience:


Experience: 10+ years in Data and /or AI Governance
Hands on experience with Collibra, IBM Watson, Credo AI, or Informatica.
Deep knowledge of AI frameworks (eg: NSW AIAF) and global AI regulations
Excellent stakeholder management with a proven ability to partner with diverse functions
Strong ability to design adoption strategies that stick

Cyber Governance Pty Ltd partners with some of Australia’s most recognisable and trusted organisations to deliver capability that genuinely matters. We are currently supporting a South Australian household name in the public utility sector as they embark on a major AI‑driven transformation and are seeking an experienced AI Technical Lead to play a pivotal role in shaping, building and deploying next‑generation AI solutions. 

This is a rare opportunity to work at scale, influence strategy, and see your work deliver real‑world outcomes for millions of people. 

The Opportunity  

What You’ll Be Doing 

Design and develop AI Proofs‑of‑Concept (PoCs) focused on LLMs and Generative AI use cases 

Guide the transition of validated PoCs into scalable, secure and production‑ready solutions 

Fine‑tune and adapt pre‑trained models to meet enterprise and operational requirements 

Build and maintain robust pipelines for model training, evaluation, deployment and lifecycle management 

Design and implement Agentic and multi‑agent AI workflows using modern orchestration frameworks and best practices 

Collaborate closely with senior business and IT stakeholders to ensure AI initiatives align with organisational priorities 

Lead delivery teams and streams of work, driving high standards of quality, governance and reliability 

Monitor, optimise and enhance deployed AI solutions to ensure ongoing performance and value 

Support solution architecture discussions and pre‑sales activities as a trusted technical advisor 

What You’ll Bring 

Essential Experience & Skills 

5+ years of professional experience in core Machine Learning or Data Science roles, building models and systems prior to the recent Generative AI wave 

Demonstrated experience applying that ML foundation to Generative AI and LLM‑based systems in enterprise or consulting environments

Proven experience deploying AI or LLM solutions into live, scalable production environments (beyond demos or exploratory PoCs) 

Strong expertise in machine learning and deep learning frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch) 

Advanced Python skills and experience building and integrating enterprise‑grade APIs 

Practical experience with LLM fine‑tuning, orchestration and Generative AI tooling 

Solid knowledge of cloud‑based AI platforms (AWS SageMaker, Azure AI, Google Vertex AI) 

Proven ability to translate complex business problems into robust, scalable technical solutions 

Strong stakeholder management skills, including engagement with senior executives 

You’ll Stand Out If You Have 

Experience delivering AI solutions in regulated or critical infrastructure environments 

Exposure to enterprise architecture, data governance or security considerations

This role is not suited to candidates who are new to AI, transitioning from adjacent other roles, or whose experience is limited to prompt engineering, short courses or experimental GenAI projects.

Job Title: Robotics Software Architect (Tier 1 Mining Global Mining OEM)
Ad Text:
About the Company
Our client is a Tier 1 global mining OEM, recognised for delivering cutting-edge technology and innovation across autonomous and heavy equipment solutions. With a strong focus on safety, productivity, and sustainability, they are leading the way in next-generation mining systems.

About the Role
We’re looking for a highly experienced Software Architect to lead the design of advanced autonomy systems for underground mining equipment (LHD machines). This is a key role where you’ll shape next-generation solutions across machine control, robotics, and semi-real-time embedded systems.
Working within a globally distributed team, you’ll define scalable, high-quality architectures aligned to a forward-looking autonomy strategy.

Key Responsibilities

Design and deliver scalable, maintainable architectures for machine control and robotic systems
Translate system-level requirements into robust software designs and specifications
Lead architecture governance including design reviews, documentation, and continuous improvement
Ensure software quality, consistency, and reuse across multiple solutions
Collaborate with product managers, system architects, and engineering teams
Drive architectural decisions, trade-offs, and documentation (ADR)
Support CI/CD pipelines and always-releasable software practices
Mentor engineers and promote best practices in modern software design
Stay current with emerging technologies in robotics, automation, and embedded systems
About You – Essential Experience

Background in machine control, robotics, automation, or sensor fusion systems
Strong expertise in real-time / embedded software development (C/C++ or similar)
Experience with modern architecture patterns (microservices, layered, component-based)
Knowledge of industrial communication protocols (CAN, Ethernet/IP, Modbus)
Exposure to autonomous systems (computer vision, ML, control systems)
Experience in Agile, DevOps, CI/CD environments
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills
Experience working with global/distributed teams
Nice to Have

Cloud-connected edge systems experience
Embedded cybersecurity knowledge
Exposure to safety-critical environments
Qualifications

Degree in Software Engineering, Computer Science, Robotics, Mechatronics, or related field
10+ years’ experience in embedded or industrial software architecture
Why Join?

Join a Tier 1 mining OEM at the forefront of autonomy and robotics
Work on cutting-edge technology shaping the future of mining
Contribute to solutions that improve safety and productivity at scale
Collaborate with high-performing, global engineering teams
Tackle complex, real-world technical challenges
Important Information

On-site role – 5 days per week in Tullamarine, Melbourne
Must have full working rights in Australia (no restrictions)
Must be currently based in the Greater Melbourne area

About the Company:

APAC is committed to weaving Diversity into our DNA.
